authority: This file is the single source of truth for numbers (GR-1). No metric appears in prose except as a citation of this file. Keystone-only writes.

objective.gate: tools/lexon_check.mjs full pass on every proposed LEXICON entry, plus 5 held-out census terms expressed by the assayer using only the proposal's pattern recipe, plus 3 covered regression entries; gateResult 8/8 (T5: zero collapses). Regime: spec-checker (real Lexon compiler is macOS-only, source unpublished; see SOURCES.md lexon-compiler-status).
objective.hardConstraint: every entry cites a SOURCES.md slug (GR-9 trace-or-delete); register discipline proven/conjectured/speculative never upgraded; no em dashes in artifact/; no C-series numbers or confidence percentages from any seat (GR-3)

baseline.how: node tools/census.mjs --freeze (rule pinned in artifact/CENSUS.json and tools/census.mjs EXCLUSIONS); coverage-debt re-derived by node tools/coverage.mjs against artifact/LEXICON.lexon.md

best.evidence: run lexr5 2026-07-12 (wf_4061aab9-983, 41 agents, status INCOMPLETE: rounds 1-3 complete, round 4 proposers lost to Fable usage-credit exhaustion mid-run, engine reported outage not exhaustion, nothing folded from it; session model swapped to Opus 4.8 for the keystone fold). OT-7 charter under three DISJOINT territories (artifact/OT7_TERRITORIES.json): 8 levers VALIDATED 8/8 all critic-classed structural, 1 MIRAGE killed structurally (K-3 instrument-charter-family lexr5.3: raw em dash in the T-172 term field, CR-10 third catch; its cast-and-infrastructure territory was already swept by the validated round-1/2 cast levers, so nothing lost). Every validated lever exercised 4-of-4 claim types in its held-out draw. Keystone fold 2026-07-12: 30 unique proposal entries (disjoint territories, one-expression-per-censusId first-validated preference; the round-2 T-049 with a recipe-sourced Network role did NOT fold, the canon-role round-1 version did) + 12 deduped assayer-authored held-out entries, all 42 re-verified GATE PASS + RELATION PASS at fold; T-117 GUID and T-167 Aether Blade folded with template-thin flags (lexr5.3 critic) since their orderings carry the term structure. node tools/coverage.mjs = 22, 189 covered, 0 failed. Residue 22 = 16 uncovered territory terms + 6 keystone-reserved C-series ids. Prior best 64 by lexr4 fold (chronicles/2026-07-12_lexr4_fold.md). Verdicts in runs/lexr5/lexr5.*/ and the engine record.

openTarget.statement: the last residue: 16 uncovered census terms and the C-series naming convention (lexr6 + operator gate). The 16 (formula: T-100 T-106 T-114 T-115 T-161; cast: T-119 T-121 T-138 T-142 T-177 T-180 T-190 T-191; zero-party: T-166 T-171 T-184) are the terms lexr5's territory sweeps did not reach in three rounds; a lexr6 over the merged charter with the same territories should close most, and any that resist after a full dry run are named-permanent residue in this file. SEPARATELY, the 6 reserved C-series bundle ids (T-195 T-196 T-197 T-198 T-199 T-203) need the operator's Phase 1.4 naming convention before they are expressible (their canon titles carry conjecture numbers and confidence percentages seats are barred from writing); recommendation stands: keystone-authored entries honestly labelled like the constitution corpus. OT-7 (merged charter, disjoint territories, the hard residue) MET by lexr5: coverage-debt 64 to 22, the census 90 percent covered, the numeric layer and universal cast rule both proven in the field.
